mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, increasing aggregate demand for corporate goods and services, which lifts S&P 500 valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during 3 consecutive calendar years, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In periods of elevated consumer credit growth, rising RSAFS levels indicate increased household leverage that fuels near-term consumption, directly supporting equity earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when RSAFS growth exceeds the 2.0% threshold for three consecutive months, indicating debt saturation rather than demand stimulation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects stronger manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, tight monetary policy suppresses equity valuations by raising the risk-free rate and compressing price-to-earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in real personal savings indicate a healthy labor market with sufficient household liquidity, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive risk-adjusted returns in a 12-month out-of-sample test, or the interaction effect reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a sharp drop in industrial production combined with rising consumer prices signals cost-push inflation that erodes corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite simultaneous drops in INDPRO and spikes in CPIAUCSL, or the forward 5-day return distribution shows no statistically significant difference from baseline (p > 0.10).
